The shape of the year matters: standalone-quarter net profit ran about 17.4 million, then 29.0 million, then 10.8 million, then 5.5 million - a strong start giving way to a near-breakeven fourth quarter (fourth-quarter profit excluding one-off items was just about 1.9 million yuan on about 276 million of revenue). So first-half profit was still up about 6.5%, then the second half collapsed and dragged the full year negative. The company is the smallest of its Chinese reducer-layer neighbours, and on the financial-quality screen it is clearly the marginal name - profit declining and accelerating downward, on a stable base.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report and interim quarterly reports
vault_ref: ZHONGDA company page / financial snapshot - profit trajectory

section_title: Cash conversion broke before the profit did
body_text: The sharper problem than the profit line is cash. Operating cash flow collapsed 85.3% to about 30.7 million yuan, from about 209.0 million a year earlier - and this ran the entire year, not just the weak back half: first-half operating cash flow was already only about 9.0 million yuan versus 108.7 million a year earlier, a 91.8% fall, while profit was still growing. The cause is a receivables blowout: trade receivables jumped to about 365.3 million yuan from about 252.7 million, roughly 45% higher, against revenue up only 6.6%, as sales shifted toward the distribution channel (distribution revenue up 27.5%) amid an industry-wide collection difficulty the company flags directly. The balance sheet moved with it: short-term borrowings roughly doubled to about 209.5 million yuan, from 6.11% to 11.31% of assets, against cash of about 92.5 million - a swing into net debt, with fixed assets and intangibles pledged as collateral for those borrowings. Cash conversion, not headline profit, is the sharper tell.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report and interim report
vault_ref: ZHONGDA company page / cash flow, receivables, net debt

section_title: Positioning, not revenue - opportunity and risk on the same page
body_text: Zhongda is the most humanoid-explicit of its Chinese reducer-layer neighbours in its own filings, mentioning humanoid robots 16 times in the annual report where one neighbour carries zero such terms. It has developed specific humanoid-joint harmonic models, ZSHF-11 and ZSHD-14, plus dexterous-hand reducers for handling small objects. But those exist at the developed or sample stage - not yet a sized revenue line. The precision-reducer business, about 24.2% of revenue, is overwhelmingly industrial-robot reducers today, and no humanoid original-equipment customer is named at primary. So the humanoid framing is a forward positioning built on a real, full-category, integrated industrial base - not humanoid revenue. The company relays a third-party demand-size projection (roughly 1 billion dollars in 2024 growing toward 15 billion by 2030) and describes itself as set to benefit; those figures are company-relayed, attributed rather than independently verified. And it lists humanoid industrialisation falling short of expectations as a formal risk factor - the industry still early, the technology route unsettled, mass-production timing uncertain. Opportunity and risk carried together.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report; demand-size figures are company-relayed
vault_ref: ZHONGDA company page / humanoid positioning and risk factor

section_title: Q1 2026: the slide continued - has it bottomed?
body_text: The deterioration extended into the first quarter of 2026: revenue up about 5.1% but net profit down 36.0% to about 11.1 million yuan, down 43.2% excluding non-recurring items, return on equity at 0.92%, and operating cash flow negative at about minus 9.8 million yuan. The company attributes the first quarter explicitly to gross-margin compression plus rising expenses. This is not distress - the FY2025 audit is a clean, standard unqualified opinion signed in April 2026, and the company remains profitable, founder-controlled, and dividend-paying. The honest read is deteriorating quality on a stable base. What is unresolved is whether the profit and cash-flow decline bottoms or is structural: profit fell through 2025 to a near-breakeven fourth quarter and fell again in the first quarter of 2026, while operating cash flow collapsed on the receivables build. Whether margin and collections stabilise, or the distribution-channel shift keeps stretching cash conversion, is the open watch item.
qualifier: from the Q1 2026 quarterly report; financial-fragility watch
vault_ref: ZHONGDA company page / open questions - deterioration trajectory

section_title: The '1+1+1' platform - built from the inside out
body_text: Zhaowei's durable position is a vertically integrated micro-drive platform, not a hand shell it buys and badges. It makes the integrated dexterous hand plus the parts inside it: micro-motors - including a 4mm brushless coreless motor in mass production - micro-gearboxes and reducers, lead screws, and the motor-control electronics. Management describes this as its '1+1+1' platform: micro-transmission system, micro-motor system, and motor-control system, sold both as integrated drive systems and as precision components, backed by a 4-42mm full-stack self-developed motor series. Independent analysis characterizes the company by name as a cross-over entrant supplying integrated dexterous-hand drive modules that combine micro-motors, reduction gearboxes, and lead screws, on cost-performance positioning leveraging that precision micro-transmission base. The value it defends sits in the drive system in the palm.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report; the cross-over-entrant characterization is independent analysis
vault_ref: ZHAOWEI company page / 1+1+1 micro-drive platform

section_title: A strong 2025, then a sharp Q1 2026 reversal
body_text: Through 2025 the business built quarter on quarter. Full-year revenue was about 1,715.5 million yuan, up 12.52%; net profit to shareholders about 254.3 million yuan, up 12.97%; operating cash flow up 28.89%; and standalone quarterly net profit accelerated across the year (roughly 54.7, then 58.6, then 68.0, then 73.1 million yuan). The nine-month revenue growth was about 18.7%. Then the first quarter of 2026 reversed hard: revenue about 357.5 million yuan, down 2.74%, and net profit about 41.0 million yuan, down 25.15% - below every quarter of 2025, with ROE down to 1.17%. This down quarter is not smoothed over. Whether it is a margin or timing blip, or the start of a slowdown in the auto and consumer core, is the central financial question; the read is to watch the first half of 2026 for whether margin and volume recover.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report and the Q1 2026 quarterly report
vault_ref: ZHAOWEI company page / FY2025 and Q1 2026 financial snapshot

section_title: Fortress balance sheet, low return on it
body_text: The balance sheet is the strongest in its China peer group. Net assets to shareholders were about 3,483 million yuan (up 7.96%) against total assets of about 4,318 million yuan, an equity ratio near 81%, with net cash, no long-term debt, and a clean unqualified audit. Much of the cash sits in wealth-management and asset-management products - the bulk of the roughly 2.0 billion yuan raised at the 2021 Shenzhen IPO. The trade-off is capital efficiency: weighted ROE was just 7.14%, depressed not because the company is unprofitable but because it is over-capitalized - about 3.48 billion yuan of equity and 4.32 billion of assets against only about 1.72 billion of revenue, with much of the proceeds in low-return wealth-management. The other side of that is a large, self-funded war chest to bankroll a robotics build-out without raising external capital - an option that is cheap to hold.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: ZHAOWEI company page / balance sheet and capital efficiency

section_title: The dexterous-hand leg - real products, unsized revenue
body_text: The dexterous-hand products are genuine and shipping or sampling, with named specifications and CES visibility: A17 (17 active degrees of freedom, finger-joint built-in drive units, for fine operations), B06 (6 degrees of freedom, linkage transmission, heavy-duty), and B20 (20 active degrees of freedom, 600g whole-hand, grip at least 30N, fingertip force 5-12N, launched at CES in January 2026), plus the ZWHAND platform and a dedicated subsidiary with 50 million yuan of registered capital. This is far beyond positioning-only engagement. But it is not a disclosed revenue line: the company reports by product type - micro-transmission about 60.1%, precision parts about 34.7%, molds and other about 5.2% - and humanoid and dexterous-hand sales are embedded, not broken out, so on the evidence the leg is still small. Management frames humanoid as the strategic frontier, aiming to leverage its micro-motor-drive platform to establish a position in humanoid-robot core components for embodied AI. The honest read: a real, product-backed option on the dexterous-hand layer, not yet a sized business; mass production is still ramping.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report; the products are disclosed but no dexterous-hand revenue line is broken out
vault_ref: ZHAOWEI company page / dexterous-hand leg

section_title: Route-risk - the winning hand architecture is unsettled
body_text: A dexterous hand is the densest mechatronic object on a humanoid - dozens of degrees of freedom in a palm-sized package - and independent analysis scores the module as a high-value humanoid subsystem on content value plus technology and manufacturing barriers. But the same analysis gives it the lowest certainty of technology choice in its peer set: the winning hand architecture is unsettled - tendon-driven versus linkage versus direct-drive, how many degrees of freedom, where the motors sit - so there is genuine route-risk, and that unsettledness is the layer's defining feature. Zhaowei's response is to hedge with a product range rather than bet a single design (A17, B06, B20), which is a rational answer to the caveat but does not remove it: a winning design the company does not hold could strand part of that range. The independent framing is a cross-over entrant on cost-performance, not a premium leader, and the coreless-motor half of the actuator is commoditizing with a low moat.
qualifier: independent analysis; certainty-of-technology-choice and cross-over-entrant framing are third-party estimates
vault_ref: ZHAOWEI company page / dexterous-hand architecture route-risk

section_title: The micro-drive core the option sits on
body_text: The frontier sits on a real, diversified, profitable micro-drive base of about 1.72 billion yuan in revenue - outside AI-datacenter scope, but the source of the cash and the platform competency. The '1+1+1' platform combines micro-transmission (gearboxes and reducers), micro-motors, and motor-control, sold as integrated drive systems and as precision components. End-markets are smart auto (the largest - central-control screen drives, e-parking-brake actuators, with Bosch, BYD, and Changan named as core customers), consumer and medical (eyewear adjustment, surgical staplers, insulin pumps, lawn-mower-robot drives), advanced industrial (antenna and feeder, valve actuators), and robotics. The mix is described as low-cyclicality, and revenue is heavily domestic - domestic sales about 88.3% of the total (up 15.44%) versus overseas about 11.7% (down 5.52%). Because it makes the precision-mechanical micro-drive half of the actuator rather than magnet-motors, it is not a direct rare-earth-magnet consumer.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: ZHAOWEI company page / core micro-transmission and drive business

section_title: The grinding base is in production, not on a slide
body_text: The roller-screw layer of the humanoid supply chain is, at bottom, a precision-grinding problem - grinding is what gates supply, which is exactly what makes it a pricing-power bottleneck. Hengli's linear-drive project is in production: capacity for about 70,000 sets a year of precision-ground ball screws, plus about 360,000 metres a year of high-precision rails, serving 300-plus customers. The Chinese term used in the filing, 精密磨削 (precision grinding), maps directly onto the grinding-gated bottleneck. The point worth keeping straight: this is a producing capability, not an announced facility. The planetary roller screws themselves - the humanoid-relevant part - are earlier, at sampling and initial mass production, and the linear-drive products are described as serving mid-high-end industrial equipment, not a named humanoid program.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: HENGLI company page / precision-grinding linear-drive build-out

section_title: The most credible entrant, the most diluted humanoid signal
body_text: As a humanoid name Hengli is the most diluted of the China humanoid names, and I'm direct about it. In the 183-page annual, 液压 (hydraulic) appears 311 times, 丝杠 (screw) 3 times, and 人形 (humanoid) zero; 机器人 (robot) appears twice, in a board-member bio and a business-license line, not in operations. The planetary-roller-screw business is bundled into an accessories-and-castings line that is only about 3.5% of revenue (about ¥385 million), and the humanoid-relevant screws are a just-started fraction of that. The humanoid end-market is never named. So the honest shape is: a credible precision-grinding capability confirmed at the primary record, with an early, unsized, unnamed humanoid roller-screw sliver sitting on top of it - optionality, not yet a business.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: HENGLI company page / humanoid-dilution honest verdict

section_title: Why the grinding credential is believed - a monopoly already broken
body_text: The credibility does not rest on a forecast; it rests on a track record. Hengli broke the international-brand monopoly in high-end excavator hydraulics, filling the domestic gap in high-end super-large-excavator pumps (the filing's phrasing: filled the domestic high-end super-large-excavator-pump gap, further breaking the foreign-brand monopoly), with mid-large excavator pumps and valves up about 40% in FY2025. Having demonstrably mastered a comparable precision-machining bottleneck against entrenched foreign incumbents is the reason independent research names Hengli on the roller-screw layer over smaller, more focused entrants. The capability travels because precision grinding gates both the hydraulics it already dominates and the roller screws it is now sampling.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: HENGLI company page / oligopoly-breaking hydraulics pedigree

section_title: A hydraulics blue-chip, not a humanoid one
body_text: The business is the highest-quality of the China humanoid names - and it is a hydraulics story, not a humanoid one. FY2025 revenue was about ¥10.94 billion, up 16.5% and the first year above ¥10 billion; net profit to shareholders about ¥2.73 billion, up 9.0%; net margin around 25%; weighted return on equity 16.6%. The balance sheet is a fortress: cash of about ¥8.87 billion against short-term borrowings of about ¥13.5 million and long-term borrowings of ¥20 million, so it is essentially debt-free with net cash near ¥8.85 billion. Core hydraulics - cylinders plus pumps, valves and motors - is about 88% of revenue. None of this strength is humanoid-driven; it is a construction-machinery blue-chip in a cyclical upswing.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report (audited, unqualified opinion)
vault_ref: HENGLI company page / FY2025 financial snapshot

section_title: Real recovery, cyclical, margins compressing
body_text: The upswing is real on volume and it comes with a margin headwind - both halves are true. Revenue accelerated as the construction-machinery cycle turned up: 9-month 2025 revenue rose about 12.3%, then Q1 2026 revenue rose about 32.5%, on downstream demand recovery and rising sector sentiment. But margins are under pressure: Q1 2026 revenue jumped about 32.5% while earnings rose only about 6.5%; full-year net profit (up 9.0%) lagged revenue (up 16.5%); and full-year operating cash flow fell about 27% as the ramp absorbed working capital. Earnings track the excavator cycle, which is currently a tailwind but is the swing factor - so the honest read is a genuine cyclical volume recovery with a margin-and-mix headwind to watch.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual and Q1 2026 quarterly filings
vault_ref: HENGLI company page / cyclical recovery and margin compression

section_title: The roller-screw bottleneck, and its two-sidedness
body_text: A planetary roller screw converts a motor's rotation into linear motion in the load-bearing joints - knee, ankle, arm - where threaded rollers take walking shock loads that balls cannot. Independent research scores it 14 out of 18 and calls it the most acute physical bottleneck alongside harmonic gears, citing a narrow supplier base, long lead times and limited substitution; a humanoid uses roughly 14 of them, and the roller-screw linear joint is put at 20-30% of high-spec component cost. Its capacity-flexibility is scored the lowest possible, 1, precisely because precision grinding gates supply. That cuts both ways for Hengli, which sits on both sides: it benefits if grinding stays scarce, and, as one of the names scaling grinding capacity, it also contributes to eroding that pricing power if capacity scales faster than expected. This is a forward analyst framing, not a filed result.
qualifier: independent analysis; forward framing, not a filed result
vault_ref: HENGLI company page / roller-screw bottleneck score and falsifier

section_title: What actually pays the bills: about 99% automotive bearings
body_text: Start with what Sling actually is. About 99% of its revenue is automotive bearings, and its stated identity remains to become a globally competitive auto-bearing maker - robot components is an expansion, not a reinvention. In the full year, brake-system bearings were 620.21 million yuan, or 78.69% of revenue (up 1.49%, gross margin 32.41%); transmission-system bearings 101.68 million yuan, 12.90% (up 11.93%); power-system bearings 47.99 million yuan, 6.09%; non-automotive bearings 11.53 million yuan, 1.46%. The products are wheel-hub bearing units, tapered, clutch, tensioner and heavy-truck bearings applied to vehicle brake, power and transmission systems. Overall gross margin was around 32% - high for an auto-parts maker, reflecting an aftermarket and hub-bearing mix and an export-weighted book.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: SLING company page / core automotive wheel-hub-bearing business

section_title: The bearing-first ladder: reducer bearings, then the reducer, then the module
body_text: Sling enters the rotary-actuator layer from the precision bearing it already owns, and it climbs the layer inside-out - three products, deepest-first by maturity. First, reducer-specific bearings: cross-roller and flexible bearings that sit inside precision reducers and high-precision rotary joints, carrying radial, axial and overturning-moment loads - this is in production and is Sling's home discipline. Second, the harmonic reducer itself - the strain-wave gear with its flexspline, circular spline and wave generator - for which the company states it completed second-phase production by period-end. Third, the integrated actuator module, combining a harmonic reducer, a frameless motor, a servo driver, a magnetic encoder and a brake into a complete rotary actuator - this is research-reserve complete but not yet in production. The reducer-specific cross-roller bearing is the same sub-component the Japanese strain-wave-gear pioneer's precision arm also makes.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: SLING company page / bearing-first robot-components pivot

section_title: Healthy books: net cash, a strong margin, cash flow above profit
body_text: On the financial-quality screen Sling resolves to the sound end. Full-year revenue was 788.17 million yuan, up 1.79%. Net profit to shareholders was 172.60 million yuan - a net margin near 22% - on a gross margin around 32%. Operating cash flow was 185.81 million yuan, which exceeds net profit. The balance sheet is net cash: cash of 1,076.81 million yuan (about 43% of total assets) against a single 223.56 million yuan long-term bank loan and no material short-term debt, for a debt ratio of 27.18% and equity around 73%. Receivables were 266.8 million yuan, 98.16% of them aged under one year, with export receivables covered by short-term export-credit insurance. The audit is a standard unqualified opinion, signed in April 2026.
qualifier: from the FY2025 audited annual report
vault_ref: SLING company page / financial-quality assessment

section_title: Margin compression, not a demand problem
body_text: The soft numbers deserve to be stated plainly and then framed correctly. Net profit fell 9.17% for the full year and 15.55% in the first quarter of 2026. But revenue kept growing the whole time - up 8.75% in the first half and up 8.72% in the first quarter of 2026 - while profit fell, so the pressure is on margin and expense, not on volume. Standalone-quarter net profit ran 46.5, then 52.7, then 40.6, then 32.8 million yuan: profit grew through the first half, then the full year turned down because the fourth quarter was weak (down roughly 38% year-over-year). Full-year gross margin was actually roughly flat (up 0.16 point), which points the decline below the gross line - operating expenses from the capacity build-out and rising research spend. Weighted return on equity has stepped down over three years, from 18.15% to 11.45% to 9.64%. The watch-item is the cycle, not the balance sheet.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report and the H1 2025 and Q1 2026 interim reports
vault_ref: SLING company page / revenue-resilient, margin-compressing shape

section_title: Export-heavy, and building a US plant against tariffs
body_text: Sling is the most export-weighted of its Chinese peers: about 71% of revenue is overseas. In the full year, overseas revenue was 557.14 million yuan (70.69%, up 5.24%) against domestic revenue of 231.04 million yuan (29.31%, down 5.67%), with customers across the US, Canada, Germany, the UK, France, Italy, Hungary, Poland, Turkey, Japan, Korea and Singapore. That export weighting carries trade and tariff exposure, and Sling is responding with localization: it runs two production bases in Zhejiang Xinchang and Thailand Ayutthaya, completed a third-phase Thailand expansion in 2025, and is building a new plant in Michigan in the US, where foundations are complete and production is pending. In November 2025 it also agreed to acquire a 24.34% stake in a precision-bearing maker, framed as deepening the precision-bearing business - a tuck-in, not a reducer or actuator acquisition.
qualifier: from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: SLING company page / export footprint and US plant

section_title: The honest gap: humanoid is not named in the filings
body_text: The widest gap in Sling's story is between the label and the disclosure. Across its filings the words for 'humanoid' and 'dexterous hand' do not appear at all. The robot framing is industrial: high-precision components for core motion joints, high-end smart-terminal equipment, machine tools, medical devices and automation lines. The company describes the field itself as still in its nascent stage. The harmonic-reducer and robot-components leg is genuine and partly in production, but it is not yet a broken-out revenue line - the roughly 99% automotive-bearing split absorbs it - so it is not yet a sized business. An outside sell-side map places Sling on a rotary-actuator slide, but that is a forward read on a real, industrial-framed, pre-material base; Sling itself is a profitable listed auto-bearing maker with an emerging reducer leg. Whether that leg becomes a separately-disclosed business, and whether owning the bearing inside the reducer wins a cost or quality edge, are the open questions.
qualifier: open questions and disclosure reading from the FY2025 annual report
vault_ref: SLING company page / humanoid-not-named and open questions

section_title: The refinancing cliff - the honest centerpiece
body_text: The Chapter 11 Exit Notes - $25.3M net (about $28.1M gross), maturing December 7, 2026, at a floating rate and secured on substantially all assets - are classified a current liability. The 10-K states the company 'has not yet secured additional financing to repay the Exit Notes at their maturity' and that 'there is no assurance that we will be able to repay or refinance the Exit Notes at or prior to their maturity date.' Cash of $28.9M barely covers the balance, and operating cash flow is negative - so repayment depends on a refinancing, an uplisting-enabled raise, or fresh capital. This is the single most important fact about the company.
qualifier: from the FY2026 10-K
vault_ref: CEPL page / Exit-Notes maturity

section_title: FY2026 - the first profitable year in ~38 years
body_text: Total revenue was $106.0M, up 23.9% from $85.6M. Net income was $2.8M - the first full fiscal year of positive net income in the company's roughly 38-year history. Gross margin was 32% (up 480 basis points from 27.2%), and adjusted EBITDA was $15.9M, roughly double the prior year's $7.9M. One caveat to read carefully: the reported diluted EPS of $(3.21) is not an operating loss - it reflects a $69.6M non-cash 'deemed dividend' from preferred-share accretion; ex-accretion basic EPS was +$0.14.
qualifier: from the FY2026 10-K
vault_ref: CEPL page / FY2026 turnaround

section_title: Going concern removed, controls remediated - recently
body_text: The FY2026 10-K carries no going-concern qualification; the earlier December-2025-quarter filing still carried substantial-doubt language ('substantial doubt about the Company's ability to meet its obligations'). The doubt was lifted between that quarter and year-end, driven by a March 2026 strategic investment that lifted cash from $15.2M to $28.9M - so the removal is real but recent, and it does not resolve the December 2026 maturity. The 10-K also concludes disclosure controls were effective and that the 'previously reported material weakness has been fully remediated. There are no remaining material weaknesses.' A clean audit opinion was issued.
qualifier: from the FY2026 10-K
vault_ref: CEPL page / going-concern removal

section_title: The data-center angle - real direction, unproven revenue
body_text: The data-center opportunity appears in the 10-K as an emerging market and a risk factor, not a revenue line. Management is building toward it: an 800-volt DC microturbine meant to interface with a next-generation AI-chip power standard, in pilot with unnamed 'data center infrastructure companies' - with 'nothing signed at this time.' A separate 'Energy Surplus' reference design integrates into a data center's power ring and pairs waste-heat recovery with waterless cooling. Management describes the pipeline as 'doubled' and better-qualified. But there is no data-center revenue, no named customer, and nothing signed - this is optionality, not a proven thesis.
qualifier: from the FY2026 10-K and earnings call
vault_ref: CEPL page / data-center optionality

section_title: The financial profile - thin GAAP, strong cash
body_text: FY2025 total revenue was $3,427.2M, up 28% from $2,684.3M. The first quarter of 2026 was the first to exceed $1 billion, at $1,006.4M, up 31%, with total ARR above $4B. Free cash flow is strong: $914.7M in FY2025 (about a 27% margin) and $289.1M in the quarter (about 29%). GAAP net income stays thin - $107.7M in FY2025, a 3.1% margin - because of heavy stock-based compensation and amortization, so free cash flow is the cleaner read on the business. Non-GAAP operating margin runs about 22%.
qualifier: from the 10-K and 10-Q filings
vault_ref: DDOG page / financial snapshot

section_title: AI-native concentration - the honest counterweight
body_text: The AI-native customer cohort - which includes the largest customer - represented roughly 7 percentage points of fourth-quarter 2025 year-over-year revenue growth. The cohort is the leading companies in foundational models, code-generation tools, and vertical AI; it includes 22 customers spending over $1M a year and 5 spending over $10M. The annual report states these customers 'rapidly increased their usage of our product and then optimized or may in the future optimize their usage or fail to renew.' Management applies 'a higher degree of conservatism to our largest customer.' The 2022 cloud-optimization episode shows usage-based revenue can halve within quarters when customers throttle spend.
qualifier: from the annual report risk factors and the Q1 2026 earnings call
vault_ref: DDOG page / AI-native concentration

section_title: The consumption dial - both ways
body_text: The first quarter of 2026 included the strongest sequential existing-customer usage growth since the first quarter of 2022; the finance chief called the $53M quarter-over-quarter revenue add a record for a first quarter. Because Datadog bills on usage, this reads deployed cloud and AI infrastructure being consumed directly. Revenue excluding the AI-native customers re-accelerated to the mid-20s percent (from 23% the prior quarter and 19% a year earlier), and net revenue retention re-accelerated to the low-120s percent - which suggests the core base is broadening, not just AI-startup adoption. The same usage model means a pullback would show here first.
qualifier: from the Q1 2026 earnings call
vault_ref: DDOG page / consumption dial

section_title: Usage-based billing and the agent tailwind
body_text: On the call the chief executive framed the model plainly: the platform is usage-based, so it does not really matter where the usage comes from - a human or an AI agent - because it bills the activity itself, and the company sees a large increase in agent-driven usage. That makes agent proliferation a tailwind to the billed unit rather than a threat to it. Platform adoption is deepening: 56% of customers use four or more products, 35% use six or more, and 20% use eight or more, across a portfolio of 26 products.
qualifier: from the Q1 2026 earnings call
vault_ref: DDOG page / usage model

section_title: Winning hyperscalers on silicon heterogeneity
body_text: Datadog is winning some hyperscaler business: on the call the chief executive noted hyperscalers 'typically have a culture of building everything themselves... yet they come to us to replace some of the things they were using before.' As hyperscalers deploy heterogeneous custom silicon - such as AWS Trainium and Graviton, Google TPUs, and Microsoft Maia - monitoring mixed fleets gets harder, since 'traditional monitoring tools fail on the custom silicon,' so a neutral cross-fleet observability layer becomes more valuable. Datadog launched GPU Monitoring and is expanding from inference into training workloads as a market.
qualifier: from the Q1 2026 earnings call
vault_ref: DDOG page / silicon heterogeneity
